betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Soyuz-FG Rocket§f

§aThe primary launch vehicle for all satellites and Gerald The Construction Android!§f

The §bSoyuz-FG§f is an immensely powerful Soviet rocket that serves as the primary launch vehicle for all satellites and orbital modules. It can be built during the nuclear age but is essential for endgame progression.

§6Assembly Recipe§f (Assembly Machine):
Titanium Shell×32 + Rubber Bar×64 + Solid Fuel (Rocket Propellant)×64 + Small Thruster×12 + Medium Thruster×12 + Control Unit×4 + Low-Density Element×32
→ Assembly Factory → Soyuz-FG

§6Launch Platform§f:
The Soyuz Launch Platform (hbm:soyuz_launcher) is crafted from:
Military Grade Circuit Board + Power Saving Upgrade Mk.III + Military Grade Circuit Board
Steel Barrel + Steel Scaffold + Steel Barrel
Steel Barrel + Lithium-Ion Battery + Steel Barrel
→ Soyuz Launcher Core Component

Required materials for the platform:
§b• §e4 stacks + 38§f blocks of concrete
§b• §e7 stacks§f of Launch Pad Scaffold Blocks
§b• §e6 stacks + 30§f blocks of Launch Pad Component Blocks

§6Launch Requirements§f:
§b• §e128,000 mB§f of §bKerosene§f
§b• §e128,000 mB§f of §bLiquid Oxygen§f
§b• §e750 kHE§f power
§b• 30-second countdown after pressing launch

§6Payloads§f:
§b• §bSatellites§f — all satellite types must be launched on the Soyuz
§b• §bGerald The Construction Android§f — requires an Orbital Module

§cWarning§f: All satellites must be linked to a satellite control interface with the SAME frequency before launch. An unlinked satellite will go "full freedom" and be permanently lost!

§eTip§f: Triple-check that your satellite designator frequency matches the satellite before launching. The Soyuz requires significant fuel — set up automated kerosene and liquid oxygen production before attempting a launch.
